title: Codonoboea kolokensis

The plant is classified as a member of the genus Codonoboea and the family Gesneriaceae. The family Gesneriaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. intro: Codonoboea kolokensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of Borneo. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and swelling. Bioactive constituents of Codonoboea kolokensis include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Codonoboea kolokensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions that are consumed to support immune function and alleviate various health conditions.
